query: What is the business activity of company Cognition (Use the website https://www.cognition.ai/ as your starting point)?Please focus on the positioning on the value chain, the product & services and the end-markets served.Please write paragraph-style starting with [company name] is .... Maintain proper hierarchy of information and keep a neutral tone, sticking to facts and avoiding marketing lingo.Answer in English.
answer:
{"answer": "Cognition is an American artificial intelligence company headquartered in San Francisco, California, that operates in the AI-powered software development tools segment of the information technology value chain. Founded in November 2023 by Scott Wu, Steven Hao, and Walden Yan, the company positions itself at a distinct layer within the software development value chain: rather than providing code completion or suggestion tools that assist human developers line-by-line, Cognition builds autonomous AI agents capable of independently planning, writing, testing, debugging, and deploying production-ready code. This places the company at the intersection of advanced AI research and enterprise software engineering infrastructure, where its products function as an augmentation layer that handles repetitive and complex engineering tasks, enabling human engineers to focus on system architecture and strategic decision-making [1] [7].

The company's flagship product is Devin, an autonomous AI software engineer that integrates directly into an organization's existing codebase and development tooling stack. Devin was first demonstrated in March 2024, reached general availability in December 2024, and has undergone successive major releases: Devin 2.0 (April 2025), which introduced a purpose-built integrated development environment for human-AI collaboration; Devin 2.1 (May 2025); and Devin 2.2 (February 2026) [28]. The product line has since expanded into several form factors: Devin Desktop (launched June 2026), which combines the Windsurf IDE with the Devin Cloud agent and an Agent Command Center for local development; Devin CLI for terminal-to-cloud workflows; and Devin Enterprise for organizational deployments [28]. In July 2025, Cognition acquired Windsurf, an AI-powered agentic IDE that at the time of acquisition served at least 350 enterprise customers and approximately $82 million in annualized recurring revenue. This acquisition gave Cognition coverage across the full spectrum of developer workflows, from AI-assisted coding within an IDE to fully autonomous software engineering [15]. Additional products include DeepWiki, a tool that generates interactive, Wikipedia-style documentation for any public GitHub repository, launched in May 2025 [18]; Cognition for Government, an offering tailored to the security and compliance requirements of federal agencies; and FrontierCode, a research initiative focused on evaluating AI code quality beyond correctness benchmarks, announced in June 2026 [28]. The company also offers specialized capabilities such as Auto-Triage for incident monitoring and response, Devin Review for pull request assessments, an MCP Marketplace for connecting external servers, and SWE-Check and SWE-grep for bug detection and context retrieval [28].

Cognition operates a software-as-a-service (SaaS) subscription model with tiered pricing. As of April 2026, self-serve plans include Free, Pro, Max, Teams, and Enterprise tiers, with usage measured through Agent Compute Units (ACUs). The company also launched an AI Productivity Guarantee in June 2026, committing to fund up to $10 million in usage if Devin fails to deliver equivalent engineering value for enterprise customers [28]. A Devin Open Source Initiative provides free ACUs to open-source maintainers [28]. The company has raised over $1 billion in venture capital, with a reported valuation of $26 billion as of May 2026 [28].

In terms of end-markets served, Cognition targets both individual developers and large enterprises across multiple industry verticals. Named enterprise customers span automotive (Mercedes-Benz, which is deploying Devin and Windsurf across its global engineering organization for legacy modernization and cloud-native development), financial services and banking (Goldman Sachs, Itaú, Nubank, which used Devin to refactor a multi-million-line monolith ETL system achieving reported 8x engineering time efficiency gains and 20x cost savings), IT services and consulting (Cognizant and Infosys, both partnering to deploy Devin across their engineering organizations and client bases), defense technology (Anduril), healthcare (Athena Health), and fintech (Ramp) [1] [12] [28]. The Cognition for Government offering specifically addresses the public sector and federal agency market, targeting the modernization of critical infrastructure with AI software engineering capabilities [28]. Additional documented use cases include COBOL modernization at Fortune 500 companies, .NET Framework to .NET Core migrations, hardware-in-the-loop and software-in-the-loop validation cycles, and incident recovery operations [28]. Geographically, Cognition has expanded beyond its San Francisco headquarters with an APAC headquarters in Singapore (opened April 2026), a London office (opened January 2026), and a Japan market launch (April 2026) [28]. The company employs approximately 200 people as of 2026 [7].
